passive.name: Serpentine Grace
passive.description: All Move Speed bonuses are more effective on Cassiopeia.

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Innate:}} {{ci|Cassiopeia}} gains {{sti|ms|{{as|Movement Speed}}}} per level, but she cannot purchase {{ii|Boots}} items.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Innate:}} '''Cassiopeia''' gains {{pp|4 to 72|color=ms}} {{sti|ms|{{as|'''bonus''' movement speed}}}}, but she cannot purchase [[Boots]].
wiki_detail.notes:
* The movement speed from ''Serpentine Grace'' is worth {{g|48}} per level, up to a maximum of {{g|864}} at level 18.
* Without other movement speed modifiers taken into account, ''Serpentine Grace'' grants '''Cassiopeia''' a total of {{pp|4+{{ccd|Cassiopeia|ms}} to 72+{{ccd|Cassiopeia|ms}}|color=ms}} {{sti|ms|{{as|movement speed}}}}.
* As {{ri|Magical Footwear}} requires boots, the rune will be replaced with {{ri|Triple Tonic}}.

Q.name: Noxious Blast
Q.description: Cassiopeia blasts an area with Poison after a brief delay, granting her increased Move Speed if she hits an enemy champion.
Q.tooltip: Cassiopeia blasts noxious gas, Poisoning enemies and dealing {{ tooltiptotaldamage }} magic damage over {{ e2 }} seconds. Hitting a champion grants Cassiopeia {{ e3 }}% Move Speed decaying over {{ e4 }} seconds.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.blurb:
{{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Cassiopeia}} blasts an area with {{tip|Poison}} after a brief delay, dealing {{as|magic damage}} over time to enemies hit.
She gains {{sti|ms|{{as|'''bonus''' movement speed}}}} if she hits an enemy {{tip|champion}}.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Cassiopeia''' creates a blast at the target location that explodes after a {{fd|0.4}}-second delay. Enemies within the blast are {{tip|poison|poisoned}} for 3 seconds, taking {{as|magic damage}} every {{ap|3/7|round=3}} seconds over the duration.
wiki_detail.leveling: {{st|Total Magic Damage|{{ap|75 to 215}} {{as|(+ 70% AP)}}|Magic Damage Per Tick|{{ap|75/7 to 215/7|round=2}} {{as|(+ {{ap|70/7|round=2}}% AP)}}}}
wiki_detail.description2: If ''Noxious Blast'' hits an enemy {{tip|champion}}, '''Cassiopeia''' gains {{sti|ms|{{as|'''bonus''' movement speed}}}} that decays over 3 seconds.
wiki_detail.notes:
* ''Noxious Blast'' always uses [[quick cast]], regardless of player settings.
* The damage inflicted by ''Noxious Blast'' is calculated as follows: damage / 7 ticks rounded down to the nearest integer.
** For example: with {{as|0 '''bonus''' AP}} ''Noxious Blast'' inflicts 75 damages over 7 ticks of damage, so 75/7 (≈10.71) deals first of all 10 damage, then (2{{times}}75/7) {{minus}} 10 (≈11.43) deals in a second time 11 damages, then (3{{times}}75/7) {{minus}} 10 {{minus}} 11 (≈11.14) deals in a third time 11 damages, and so on.
* The damage displayed for ''Noxious Blast'' DOT ([[Damage_over_time]]) always remains constant and corresponds to the previously mentioned formula: damage / 7 ticks rounded down to the nearest integer. It's important not to confuse the consistent displayed damage with the actual damage, which is updated with each tick, see [[tick and updates]].
** With the same example as above, the damage displayed is always 10.
* Occasionally, ''Noxious Blast'' may not deal the displayed damage. This discrepancy occurs when the calculated damage falls between 0.5 and 1. In such cases, rounding to the nearest integer, specifically rounding up, creates a 1-point difference. It's important to note that the damage is rounded down to the nearest integer.
** For example: with a {{iis|Blasting Wand}}, ''Noxious Blast'' shows {{ap|75 + {{as|90% of 45}} = 115,5 magic damage}} so rounded to the nearest integer {{ap|116 magic damage}}, but really deals using the formula above {{ap|115 magic damage}}.
* {{bug}} When '''Cassiopeia''' has precisely {{as|20 '''bonus''' AP}} or {{as|40 '''bonus''' AP}}. In this scenario, ''Noxious Blast'' may inflict 1 less damage than intended. Importantly, this bug occurs independently of rounding considerations.
* {{pending for test}} Enemies within the blast of ''Noxious Blast'' are {{tip|poison|poisoned}} for something between {{fd|3.2}} and {{fd|3.3}} seconds.

W.name: Miasma
W.description: Cassiopeia releases several clouds of poison, slowing, grounding, and lightly damaging enemies that pass through them. Grounded enemies cannot use Movement abilities.
W.tooltip: Cassiopeia spews venom, leaving toxic clouds for {{ e4 }} seconds. Enemies in the clouds take {{ damagepersecond }} magic damage per second, are Poisoned, Grounded, and Slowed by {{ e2 }}%.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Cassiopeia}} spews several clouds of {{tip|Poison}} that linger, continually dealing {{as|magic damage}} to enemies within. They are also {{tip|slow|slowed}} and {{tip|ground|grounded}}.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Cassiopeia''' spews forth 8 bolts of venom in an arc at the target location, creating toxic clouds at the area for 5 seconds.
wiki_detail.description2: Enemies within the clouds are {{tip|poison|poisoned}} to take {{as|magic damage}} every {{ap|5/19|round=3}} seconds and become {{tip|ground|grounded}} and {{tip|slow|slowed}} by an amount that decays over the area's duration.
wiki_detail.leveling2:
{{st|Magic Damage Per Second|{{ap|20 to 40}} {{as|(+ {{ap|15}}% AP)}}|Total Magic Damage|{{ap|100 to 200 }} {{as|(+ 75% AP)}}}}
{{st|Slow|{{ap|40 to 80}}%}}
wiki_detail.description3: ''Miasma will cast at max range if cast beyond that.''
wiki_detail.notes:
* ''Miasma's'' {{tip|slow}} and {{tip|ground}} debuffs are each marked as non-dispellable, so they are not removed by most {{tip|cleanse|cleanses}}. Each is however allowed to be removed by cleanses that '''also''' grant immunity to the debuff type, such as {{cai|Ragnarok|Olaf}}.
* If a target becomes {{tip|untargetable}} while affected by the {{tip|ground}}, the debuff will refresh to {{fd|0.25}} seconds.
* {{ai|Wind Wall|Yasuo}} will block the portion of ''Miasma'' it destroys.
* ''Miasma'' inflicts {{as|{{ap|5 to 10}}|magic damage}} {{as|(+ 3.75% AP)}} {{as|magic damage}} per tick, and in fact because there is 19 ticks and not 20 ticks, the {{as|'''total''' magic damage}} is currently not {{as|{{ap|100 to 200}} {{as|(+ 75% AP)}}|magic damage}} but {{as|{{ap|95 to 190}}|magic damage}} {{as|(+ 71.25% AP)}}.
** {{bug}} Sometimes ''Miasma'' randomly inflicts only 18 ticks of damage.

E.name: Twin Fang
E.description: Cassiopeia lets loose an attack that deals increased damage to Poisoned targets and heals her for a percentage of the damage dealt. If the target dies from this attack, Cassiopeia regains Mana.

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Cassiopeia}} launches an attack at the target enemy that deals {{as|magic damage}}. If this attack kills the target, the {{sti|{{as|mana cost}}}} is refunded.
wiki_detail.blurb2: Against a {{tip|Poison|poisoned}} target, the attack deals increased damage and {{tip|heal|heals}} her.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Cassiopeia''' launches her fangs at the target enemy that deal {{pp|52 to 120|color=magic damage}} {{as|(+ 10% AP)}} {{as|magic damage}}. If this kills the target, ''Twin Fang's'' {{as|{{sti|mana|mana cost}}}} is refunded.
wiki_detail.description2: Against a {{tip|poison|poisoned}} target, ''Twin Fang'' deals {{as|'''bonus''' magic damage}} and {{tip|heals}} '''Cassiopeia'''. The heal is reduced by 75% against {{tip|minions}} and small and medium {{tip|monsters}}.
wiki_detail.leveling2:
{{st|Bonus Magic Damage|{{ap|20 to 100}} {{as|(+ 55% AP)}}|Total Enhanced Damage|{{pp|52 to 120}} (+ {{ap|20 to 100}}) {{as|(+ {{ap|55+10}}% AP)}}}}
{{st|Heal|{{as|{{ap|10 to 16}}% AP}}|Reduced Heal|{{as|{{ap|10*0.25 to 16*0.25}}% AP}}}}
wiki_detail.target range: {{tip|cr|icononly = true}} {{tt|700|BUG: Displayed range, 765, is slightly larger than the real range}}
wiki_detail.notes:
* Upon reaching the target, ''Twin Fang'' will wait for an ongoing {{ai|Noxious Blast|Cassiopeia}} to explode before dealing its damage.
* ''Twin Fang's'' damage against poisoned targets:
** When maxed first: {{pp|52+20;56+20;60+40;64+40;68+60;72+60;76+80;80+80;84+100 to 120+100}} {{as|(+ 70% AP)}}.
** When maxed second: {{pp|52+20;56+20;60+20;64+20;68+20;72+20;76+20;80+40;84+40;88+60;92+60;96+80;100+100 to 120+100}} {{as|(+ 70% AP)}}.
** When maxed last: {{pp|52+20 to 100+20;104+40;108+60;112+60;116+80;120+100}} {{as|(+ 70% AP)}}.
* ''Twin Fang's'' cast indicator is incorrectly adding her own radius to the range like an {{tip|edge range}} ability, and therefore slightly larger than the actual cast range ''Twin Fang'' can cast at.
{{Unit-targeted cancel conditions}}

R.name: Petrifying Gaze
R.description: Cassiopeia releases a swirl of magical energy from her eyes, stunning any enemies in front of her that are facing her and slowing any others with their back turned.
R.tooltip: Cassiopeia unleashes a petrifying gaze, dealing {{ rdamage }} magic damage and Stunning enemies facing her for {{ rccduration }} seconds. Enemies facing away are Slowed by {{ rslowpercent }}% decaying over the same duration instead.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Cassiopeia}} blasts in a cone, dealing {{as|magic damage}} to enemies hit and {{tip|stun|stunning}} any that are facing her, while {{tip|slow|slowing}} any others with their back turned.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Cassiopeia''' blasts enemies in a cone in the target direction, dealing {{as|magic damage}} to enemies struck within and {{tip|slow|slowing}} them by 40% for 2 seconds. Enemies with their facing direction towards her are instead {{tip|stun|stunned}} for the same duration.
wiki_detail.effect radius: {{tip|cr|icononly = true}} {{tt|850|950 but starts 100 units behind Cassiopeia. Because of this, range is slightly greater laterally}}
wiki_detail.notes:
* '''Cassiopeia''' will turn to face the target direction over the cast time.
{{Effect at cast time end}}
** ''Petrifying Gaze's'' target direction will change if '''Cassiopeia's''' facing direction changes during the cast time (i.e. through {{tip|airborne|knockbacks}}).
* The facing direction of champions whose abilities/animations cause them to lock their facing or spin is always the direction they are moving in. For abilities that cause the champion to spin in place (e.g. {{ai|Death Lotus|Katarina}}, {{ai|Judgment|Garen}}) it will factor the direction they were facing on cast.
** {{cai|R|Lucian}} is an exception and his facing direction is actually considered to be the direction he is facing.
